hook: ChatGPT gave me a 9-step plan when I asked for help with one email.
caption:
ChatGPT gave me a 9-step plan when I asked for help with one email.

I didn't ask for a framework.
I didn't ask for bullet points.
I definitely didn't ask it to "consider my audience's perspective" as step 4.

I just needed the email to not sound passive-aggressive.

This is the thing nobody talks about: AI isn't bad at writing. It's bad at knowing *how you think about* writing.

Some people want the full breakdown. Walk me through it, explain the reasoning, show your work.

Other people (hi) want one clean answer and zero explanation unless they ask.

The AI doesn't know which one you are. So it defaults to "more is safer." And you get the 9-step email plan.

BootFile fixes this at the root. It builds a profile of how your brain actually works — do you want options or a decision? Context or just the answer? Formal tone or write it like a human?

Then every AI response starts from *that* instead of from a generic average of all users everywhere.

Your AI should already know you want the short version.

Link in bio to build your profile.
